name: rich
summary: Render rich text, tables, progress bars, syntax highlighting, markdown and more to the terminal

story.problem: Terminal output in Python is stuck in the 1970s by default — monochrome text, manual string formatting for tables, and no way to show progress without writing your own ANSI escape sequences. Rich changed the expectation of what a well-crafted CLI tool should look like.
story.mental_model: Rich is a markup-over-print-statements system. Use rich.print() instead of print() and add [bold red]text[/bold red] markup in your strings. For structured output, create Table or Panel objects and pass them to a Console. Under the hood, Rich auto-detects whether the terminal supports colour and gracefully degrades.
story.when_to_use: Use Rich in any CLI tool or long-running script where you want readable output: progress bars for downloads or processing loops, syntax-highlighted code, formatted tables, structured log output, or pretty-printed Python objects for debugging.
story.when_not_to_use: Rich adds 1-2MB to your package size and a startup cost. For library code that should be output-agnostic, stick to logging. For applications deployed purely as web services with no terminal interaction, Rich adds nothing.

alternatives.name: colorama
alternatives.reason: Lighter weight cross-platform colour support; less feature-rich but smaller footprint

alternatives.name: tqdm
alternatives.reason: Focused purely on progress bars — simpler to use for that single use case

alternatives.name: textual
alternatives.reason: Full TUI framework built on Rich — use it when you need interactive terminal apps, not just output

nodes.label: rich
nodes.summary: Import from rich: `from rich import print`, `from rich.console import Console`.

nodes.label: Console
nodes.summary: Main output object. `c = Console(); c.print('[bold red]Hello[/]')`. Controls width, file, markup.

nodes.label: Rich Markup
nodes.summary: BBCode-like tags: [bold], [italic], [red], [link=url], [bold magenta on white]. Nesting supported.

nodes.label: Table
nodes.summary: Render data as terminal tables. Add columns/rows, set styles, alignment, borders.

nodes.label: Progress (progress bars)
nodes.summary: Live progress bars with spinners. track() wraps iterables. Custom columns.

nodes.label: Syntax
nodes.summary: Syntax-highlighted code blocks via Pygments. `Syntax(code, 'python', theme='monokai')`.

nodes.label: Traceback
nodes.summary: Beautiful exception tracebacks with local variable values. `install()` to replace default.

nodes.label: Layout primitives (Panel, Padding, Group)
nodes.summary: Panel wraps content in a box. Padding adds whitespace. Group stacks renderables.

nodes.label: RichHandler (logging integration)
nodes.summary: from rich.logging import RichHandler. Add to logging.basicConfig for pretty log output.

nodes.label: rich.inspect()
nodes.summary: Introspect any Python object: shows attrs, methods, docstrings in a nicely formatted panel.
